question.title: Consecutive Login Days
question.desc: Find all users who have logged in for at least 3 consecutive days. Output the user id and the first day of their longest streak.
question.solution: WITH ranked_logins AS ( SELECT user_id, login_day, DATE_TRUNC('day', login_day) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY login_day) AS day_rank FROM logins ) SELECT user_id, MIN(login_day) AS first_login_day FROM ranked_logins GROUP BY user_id, day_rank HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3;
question.tips: Consider using window functions to track consecutive sequences.
hints: Look into using window functions like ROW_NUMBER() or RANK() to track the sequence of logins.
You might need to create a temporary result set (CTE) to calculate the day ranks for each user before filtering for the longest streaks.
Use DATE_TRUNC('day', login_day) - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY login_day) to calculate the day rank, then group by user_id and day_rank to find sequences of 3 or more days.

tests.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS logins; CREATE TABLE logins (user_id INT, login_day DATE); INSERT INTO logins (user_id, login_day) VALUES (4, '2022-02-01'), (4, '2022-02-02'), (4, '2022-02-03'), (4, '2022-02-04'), (5, '2022-02-01');

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS logins; CREATE TABLE logins (user_id INT, login_day DATE); INSERT INTO logins (user_id, login_day) VALUES (6, '2022-03-01'), (6, '2022-03-02'), (7, '2022-03-01'), (7, '2022-03-02'), (7, '2022-03-03');
